name: Biodiversity Monitor
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure AI Vision
services.purpose: Species identification from camera trap images, drone aerial surveys, and underwater cameras — custom models for local fauna classification

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for ecological analysis — population trend interpretation, habitat health assessment, conservation priority scoring, and natural language survey reports

services.name: Azure IoT Hub
services.purpose: Ingest data from camera traps, acoustic sensors, weather stations, water quality monitors, and GPS wildlife collars

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Species observation records, population counts, habitat assessments, sensor metadata, conservation action logs, geospatial sighting data

enterprise.note: Multi-region, analytical store for trend analysis

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Camera trap image processing pipeline, acoustic file analysis trigger, sensor data aggregation, conservation alert dispatcher

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Biodiversity API — species catalog, population analytics, habitat dashboard, conservation planning, reporting engine

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: IoT device certificates, API keys, satellite provider credentials, endangered species location data encryption

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Sensor uptime, species detection accuracy, camera trap battery levels, pipeline latency, conservation alert response times

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine species observation logging and status summaries, reserve GPT-4o for complex multi-source ecological analysis and conservation planning — saves 65% on AI costs
Process camera trap images in batch during nightly uploads rather than real-time — batch processing is 50% cheaper and camera traps typically upload via satellite at scheduled intervals
Train custom Vision models for site-specific species — smaller regional models are faster and cheaper than general-purpose classification across all fauna
Use IoT Hub message routing to deduplicate and filter sensor readings at ingestion — acoustic sensors produce high volumes but only segments with animal calls need AI analysis
Store raw camera trap images in cool/archive storage after species classification — only keep classified metadata in hot storage to reduce Cosmos DB costs by 40%
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
